notes: Fiji should give full credit to English because it functions as the main official and traveler-facing language, while also recognizing Fijian and Fiji Hindi as major national languages used widely in daily life.

evidence.title: Tourism Fiji: Need to know
evidence.note: Official tourism guidance says Fiji has three official languages, English, Fijian, and Fiji Hindi.

evidence.title: Britannica: Fiji
evidence.note: Britannica describes English as an official language alongside Fijian and Hindustani varieties.

notes: Ghana should score English as the main national and traveler-facing language. Akan and other Ghanaian languages remain important in daily life, but English is the safest country-level traveler default.

evidence.title: Visit Ghana official portal
evidence.note: Ghana's official tourism platform serves international visitors directly in English, supporting English as the main travel language.

evidence.title: Britannica: Ghana
evidence.note: Britannica identifies English as the official language of Ghana and notes Akan as the largest language group.

notes: The Maldives should score Dhivehi as the official language while giving strong English credit because the tourism economy is explicitly international and official visitor systems operate broadly in English.

evidence.title: Visit Maldives official portal
evidence.note: The Maldives' official tourism authority serves all practical visitor information in English for international travelers.

evidence.title: Britannica: Maldives
evidence.note: Britannica identifies Dhivehi as the official language of the Maldives.
